parentQuestion: The Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase pump moves ___ Na⁺ ions out and ___ K⁺ ions into the cell per ...

scaffolds.q: A pump that requires ATP to move substances is an example of ___ transport.
scaffolds.explain: Active transport requires energy (ATP) to move substances AGAINST their concentration gradient. Pumps use ATP to do this uphill work — it's expensive, but sometimes necessary, darling.

scaffolds.q: Na⁺ and K⁺ are examples of ___.
scaffolds.explain: Na⁺ (sodium) and K⁺ (potassium) are ions — atoms or molecules with an electrical charge. The plus sign indicates they have a positive charge (they've lost an electron). They're charged particles zipping around your cells. Follow the breadcrumbs:.
